app.name: Reisezoom GPS Studio
app.source_missing: Source file not found: {name} — drive mounted?
modules.animator.name: Animator
modules.tourmap.name: Tour Map
tourmap.stats.empty_hint: Load a GPX file — track stats will appear here
tourmap.style.osm_disabled_body: Tour maps require a Mapbox token (for satellite & 3D). Rendering is disabled in OSM mode.
modules.geotagger.name: Geotagger
modules.heightanim.name: Elevation Animator
modules.gpxinspect.name: GPX Inspector
gpxinspect.empty: Load a GPX via the bar above — then every single track point shows up here.
gpxinspect.help: Click two points on the map one after another (anchor A green, B red). Then pick an action for the segment in between.
gpxinspect.sel_a: Anchor A set — drag the point to move it · Delete to remove · click anchor B for a range.
gpxinspect.heal_help: Automatically finds and fixes GPS outliers and gaps. Pick scope and actions, then Heal. Undo anytime.
gpxinspect.heal_need_ab: Scope 'Section A→B': set two points on the map first (A green, B red).
gpxinspect.routegaps_help: Instead of a straight line, find the real route on the path network (Mapbox, needs internet + token). Very large gaps like flights stay straight.
gpxinspect.snap_help: Snaps the whole track onto the path network via map matching. Only useful if the track really follows paths/roads.
gpxinspect.sens_help: How strict the search is. Low = only big outliers and large gaps, high = also small ones.
gpxinspect.manual_help: Click two points on the map (A green, B red), then choose an action for the section between them.
gpxinspect.ele_help: GPS elevation is noisy. Loads the elevation profile from the map; below you blend GPS and map with the slider. Needs Mapbox token + internet.
gpxinspect.heal_opt_routegaps: Snap gaps to real paths/roads (otherwise straight line)
gpxinspect.profile_tip: For 'snap gaps' and 'snap track': which path network to use.
gpxinspect.heal_run_hint: 'Snap to paths' finds the real route (Mapbox, needs internet + token). Very large gaps (e.g. flights) stay straight. Undo anytime.
gpxinspect.heal_nothing_sel: Nothing selected — tick what should be healed.
gpxinspect.help_auto: Heal gaps and outliers automatically: tick what should happen, then press 'Heal'.
gpxinspect.heal_tip: Place the points between A and B onto the straight line (position + elevation interpolated). Timestamps stay → speed becomes realistic again.
gpxinspect.fill_tip: Insert new points on the straight line between A and B (position, elevation and time interpolated).
gpxinspect.drawfill_tip: Draw the path between A and B yourself on the map (click waypoints). The gap is filled along your path with position, elevation and time.
gpxinspect.draw_started: Click on the map to draw the path.
gpxinspect.delete_tip: Remove the points between A and B entirely (cut out loops/detours). A and B stay, the line connects them directly.
gpxinspect.route_detour: The route found makes a big detour/loop — not applied. Use “Fill gap (straight line)” or “Draw & fill path”.
gpxinspect.match_all_warn: Caution: snaps the WHOLE track onto Mapbox roads and overwrites your recorded points — junctions can produce detours/loops. For gaps/outliers only, use “Heal”. Click again to confirm.
gpxinspect.fillmode_tip: How gaps are filled: 'Straight line' interpolates directly (fast). 'Walking/Cycling/Car' finds the real route on the path/road network for each gap (Mapbox, needs internet + token) — looks much more natural with many gaps. Very large gaps (e.g. flights) are filled linearly automatically.
gpxinspect.heal_done_route: Healed: %s spikes · %r gaps snapped to route, %l filled straight
gpxinspect.ele_hint: GPS elevation is noisy (climb often too high). Load the elevation profile from the map — below the map you then see both lines overlaid and blend them with the slider. The bold line is the result. Needs a Mapbox token + internet.
gpxinspect.ele_preview: Preview: %new m climb (%pct % map). Apply to save it.
gpxinspect.ele_done: Applied: %old → %new m climb (%pct % map). Now save.
gpxinspect.ele_applied_toast: Elevation applied — save below to keep it.
gpxinspect.healed: Segment smoothed — time kept, speed corrected.
gpxinspect.mm_radius_tip: How far from a path a point still snaps. Small = only very close, large = catches more GPS drift but may snap to parallel roads. (Mapbox max 50 m.)
gpxinspect.match_sel_hint: For a section that follows a path: set A and B, then let it find the route in between.
gpxinspect.match_sel_tip: Finds the actual road/path route between anchors A and B (Directions). Robust against GPS drift — no 50 m limit. Replaces the points in between with the route; time/elevation distributed. Undoable.
gpxinspect.match_all_tip: Snap the WHOLE track onto the road network. Only useful for path/road-based tracks — for off-trail hikes it can distort the trace.
gpxinspect.match_hint: Only useful if the track follows paths/roads. Needs internet + Mapbox token.
modules.overlay.name: Overlay
heightanim.intro: Creates a video that builds up your track's elevation profile live. Live preview is available now — the video render is coming in one of the next versions.
heightanim.empty_hint: Load a GPX track to see the elevation profile.
heightanim.codec.hint.h264: Standard for YouTube, web, NLE editing.
heightanim.codec.hint.h265: Up to 40% smaller file at equal quality. Perfect on Mac/iOS; on Windows with a current player.
heightanim.codec.hint.prores: Master quality for YouTube + color grading. Very large file (~5–10× MP4).
heightanim.codec.hint.alpha: Transparent background — for overlay on video footage in editing software.
heightanim.trim.hint: Restrict which track segment is animated
heightanim.done.label: Video ready.
animator.kf.world_drag_hint: 🌍 Earth sitting too low? Just drag it up with your mouse — the keyframe updates automatically when you release.
heightanim.coming_soon: Phase 2 — render pipeline is in progress. UI scaffold, live preview, and settings are already fully working.
settings.title: Settings
settings.language.help: Takes effect immediately, no restart needed.
settings.mapbox.label: Mapbox token
settings.mapbox.help_short: Leave empty to use the bundled default token. Use your own if you render heavily or share this app.
settings.mapbox.help_link: How do I get a token?
mapbox_help.title: Get your own Mapbox token
mapbox_help.intro: The app uses Mapbox for its maps. For occasional use the bundled default token works fine. For heavy use or if you share this app, you should create your own token — free and done in 2 minutes.
mapbox_help.step4: Copy the 'Default public token' — it starts with pk.eyJ…
mapbox_help.step5: Paste the token into the field above → 'Save'. Done.
mapbox_help.cc_info: ⚠️ Since mid-2026 Mapbox requires a credit card at signup — even for the free account. You will not be charged as long as you stay within the free tier (see below).
mapbox_help.tier_info: 💡 Free tier: 50,000 map loads per month — free. In practice that's enough for many many renders. With normal hobby use you'll never see a bill — you'd have to produce really heavily to hit the limit.
mapbox_help.security_info: 🔒 Public tokens are not secret (they appear in browser anyway). Still recommended: set URL restrictions in the Mapbox dashboard so your token only works from your machine.
first_run.title: Welcome — set up your maps
first_run.intro: You have two options for the maps in this app:
first_run.opt_token_desc: Satellite, 3D terrain, premium styles, hi-res renders. Free Mapbox account in 2 min.
first_run.opt_osm_desc: Works instantly, no account. But only basic map (no satellite, no 3D, no animator render).
first_run.change_later_hint: You can change this any time in settings.
animator.osm_only: In OSM mode only one map style is available. Add a Mapbox token for satellite & 3D.
animator.render_needs_token.title: Render needs a Mapbox token
animator.render_needs_token.body: Animator render is not available in OSM mode (no satellite, no 3D terrain). Add a free Mapbox token in settings.
common.clear_workspace.title: Remove loaded data (GPX / photos) — settings stay
common.clear_workspace.confirm: Clear loaded data in the „{module}" module?
common.clear_workspace.confirm_all: Clear the entire workspace? The GPX track, photos and all loaded data in every module (Animator, Tour-Map, Elevation Animator, Geotagger) will be removed.
common.clear_workspace.note: Only GPX track and photos will be removed. Settings like Mapbox token, map style, pitch etc. remain untouched.
bugreport.title: Feedback / Bug report to Marc
bugreport.intro: Copy the address + text and paste it into your mail program or webmail (Gmail/Outlook/…). If you have a local mail program installed, you can also use '📧 Open mail app'.
bugreport.label.subject: Subject
bugreport.hint: The body contains a placeholder '[hier deinen Text einfügen]' — please replace it with a short description of what you were doing. You don't need to understand the log excerpt; it helps Marc debug.
help.feedback.title: Feedback / bug report to Marc
help.feedback.body: Opens your mail client with a pre-filled log excerpt — just write something and send.
help.title: Help
help.intro: Docs & support for Reisezoom GPS Studio. All entries are also available via the macOS menu 'Help'.
help.user_guide.title: User Guide
help.user_guide.body: Step-by-step guide for Animator, Geotagger, and Tour Map.
help.mapbox.title: Set up Mapbox token
help.mapbox.body: Get a free token — required for satellite & 3D maps.
help.log.title: Open log file
help.log.body: Detailed diagnostics for errors. Handy for support requests.
help.about.title: About the app
about.title: About Reisezoom GPS Studio
about.tagline: Native cross-platform suite for GPS/GPX workflows: track animations, photo geotagging, tour maps.
about.credits: Built by Marc Arzt and Claude Code.
about.promo: Outdoor, photography & cameras by Marc – come say hi!
about.support.title: This app is free & ad-free
about.support.body: Do you like it? A small donation is hugely appreciated and keeps development going. Thank you! 🙏
about.support.todo: Donation link isn't set up yet.
about.credits.title: Open Source components
about.credits.intro: This app uses the following libraries — thanks for the great work:
animator.style.alpha_hint: Renders only the track + overlays on a transparent background. Output is a .mov file (ProRes 4444) that can be dropped directly over real footage in Premiere/Final Cut/DaVinci. Map style, 3D terrain and lighting have no effect in this mode.
animator.tours.hint: Additional tours are animated after the first one — the camera flies cinematically from one to the next.
animator.timeline.snap_tip: Snapshots the current map view (pitch / bearing / zoom) as a new keyframe at the scrubber position.
animator.timeline.play_tip: Plays the animation once at the configured duration — quick preview without rendering. Press again (or Space) to stop.
animator.kf.rotation_tooltip: World rotation around the polar axis for this keyframe. -180° to +180°. Two KFs with different values rotate the earth between them.
animator.kf.center_tooltip: Map position of this keyframe. Longitude is unwrapped: values beyond ±180° = full earth turns on the way from the previous keyframe (like Insta360). Dragging the map counts the value up automatically.
animator.rotcounter.tooltip: Live rotation counter: shows the unwrapped longitude and the number of full earth turns while you drag the map.
animator.kf.position_tooltip: World position in the viewport — X shifts horizontally, Y vertically. -50% to +50% of viewport size.
animator.timeline.trim_start_tip: Drag render start — determines where the render begins. KFs before this point are used as run-up motion.
animator.timeline.trim_end_tip: Drag render end — determines where the render finishes.
animator.overlay.stats_use_trim_tip: When active: Stats overlay shows values for the render trim range (distance, elevation, time only for the trimmed section). Otherwise: always the full-track values.
animator.overlay.show_pretrim_tip: When active: track line is drawn from the start of the track (pre-trim portion visible as background). When off: line starts at the left trim-handle.
animator.lane.cluster_tip: Click: select all properties · Drag: move them together · Right-click: delete the entire cluster
animator.timeline.cluster.tip: Click: select all properties · Drag: move them together · Right-click: delete the entire cluster
animator.timeline.dblclick_hint: Double-click on a lane (Pitch / Rotation / Zoom / Position) sets just that one property at the click position — no full cluster. Handy when you only want to animate bearing, for instance. Double-clicking the Cluster row at the top sets all 4 together as usual.
animator.timeline.fulltrack_tip: On: the full track stays visible in the preview (no trim to scrubber position). Off: track is trimmed up to the scrubber — same as the final render.
animator.timeline.kfpins_tip: On: keyframe pins (yellow dots on the map) visible — editing aid. Off: WYSIWYG mode, pins won't appear in the final render anyway.
animator.timeline.zoom.tip: Timeline zoom: Ctrl/Cmd + Mousewheel · +/− buttons · Click number to reset to 1×. At zoom > 1: Shift+Wheel pans, Shift+Drag moves the visible range.
animator.timeline.easing.title: Transition to next keyframe
animator.kf.deactivate_warn_body: You have keyframes set. Disabling the keyframe editor now will delete all keyframes and bring back classic camera control. Continue?
animator.timeline.clear_tip: Removes all camera keyframes. The Pitch/Rotation sliders become active again afterwards.
animator.timeline.gesture_hint: 💡 Tip: drag the map normally — <kbd>Cmd</kbd>+Drag (Mac) or right-click+Drag tilts it. Then click „Capture keyframe".
animator.kf.pitch_reset_tip: Reset pitch to 0° (needed for globe/world view — otherwise the map is tilted and the globe appears off-center).
animator.kf.follow_tip: On: camera stays at the GPS point of the anchor — pitch/bearing/zoom still set individually. Off: free map view (panning the map sticks).
animator.kf.follow_hint_track: Camera follows the GPS point — panning is ignored. Pitch/bearing/zoom stay individual per keyframe.
animator.kf.follow_hint_free: Free map view — the map stays where you panned it.
animator.kf.delete_hint: Quick-delete: right-click the marker in the bar (or the pin on the map) · <kbd>Del</kbd> key when the keyframe is selected.
animator.kf.hint: Tip: drag, rotate, tilt the map — then click „Update with current map view" above. Or use the sliders to fine-tune.
animator.timeline.marker.tip: Click: select + edit · Right-click: delete · Drag: move
animator.kf.enable_tooltip: Enables the camera-keyframe timeline for cinematic camera moves. Off: classic behaviour with static pitch + linear rotation sweep.
animator.kf.enable_hint: Turn this on to drive the camera animation with track-position-based keyframes. Otherwise the animation runs classically with static tilt + steady rotation.
animator.kf.empty_hint: No keyframe selected yet. Set up the map (pan + tilt) however you want, then click "📍 Drop keyframe here" under the map — or click an existing 🎥 marker.
animator.help.label.kf_delete: How do I delete a keyframe?
animator.timeline.anim_end_tip: End of the track animation, start of the hold phase. Keyframes to the right of this line move the camera while the track endpoint stays put.
animator.timeline.anim_start_tip: End of the intro phase, start of the track animation. Keyframes to the left of this line move the camera before the track starts moving.
topbar.project.no_session: Load a GPX to use projects.
topbar.project.del_msg: Really delete „{name}"? The last remaining project will automatically be restored as „Standard".
settings.mapbox.usage_hint: Opens your Mapbox dashboard in the browser. Free tier covers 50,000 map loads per month.
animator.codec.prores_hint: Studio quality for video editing — output is .mov, several hundred MB per render.
animator.style.osm_disabled_body: Without a Mapbox token only the OSM default map is available — no satellite, no 3D terrain, no style switching. Rendering is disabled as well.
animator.hide_labels.tooltip: Hides all text and icons on the map — place names, road names, POI labels. Turns the map into a clean background. Looks particularly tidy in outdoor-style animations.
animator.map_config.tooltip: Lighting, labels, boundaries — everything Mapbox lets us configure.
map_config.title: Fine-tune map
map_config.intro: Customize the map to your taste — what gets shown and which lighting mood. The preview updates after you save.
map_config.light.hint: Only affects the Standard Satellite styles — the other map styles have their own fixed lighting.
map_config.elements.admin_hint: Visible mostly on the classic styles (Outdoor, Streets, Light, Dark) — satellite styles barely show admin boundaries.
animator.shadow.tooltip: Makes the track look like a line floating above the map. With 3D terrain the shadow stays on the ground while the track is rendered 150 m above — gives a 3D look.
animator.glow.tooltip: Colored aura/halo around the track. Makes it pop against the map. Off or 0 — just the bare line. Strength controls how soft the halo is.
animator.ghost.tooltip: Ghost track: draws the whole route faintly/semi-transparent in the background while only the animated portion is drawn fully opaque on top. Handy to see where the route still goes.
animator.field.follow_inertia_tip: 0 = locked to the point (can jitter), higher = the camera trails more smoothly
animator.field.follow_height_tip: Stops the camera from bobbing up and down over mountainous terrain. 0 = camera follows the ground, 100 = fixed cruising height (stays level no matter the terrain).
animator.field.camera_follow_track_toolt: When on, the camera re-centers on the current track point each frame. Off: camera stays locked to the configured map position.
animator.field.smooth_camera_tip: Stops the camera bobbing up and down over mountainous 3D terrain. Off = classic (default). On = smooth, decoupled camera.
animator.line_style_spacing.tooltip: Multiplies the dash/dot length in the line pattern. Only active for Dashed/Dotted/Dash-Dot.
animator.overlay.timing_tip: Show this box only within this time window (video seconds). Empty / 0 = from start resp. until end.
animator.field.padding_tip: Space between the track and the image edge
animator.field.static_pins_tip: Show start and end point as markers on the map
animator.point_count.hint: Default is all points from your GPX. Drag the slider left to make the track coarser — the preview updates live. Affects render: fewer points = slightly faster. Render time depends mostly on duration × FPS × resolution.
animator.alpha.tooltip: Renders only the track + overlays on a transparent background. Output is a ProRes-4444 .mov file that can be dropped directly over real footage in Premiere/Final Cut/DaVinci.
animator.alpha.hint: Output is a .mov (ProRes 4444) — larger than MP4 but NLE-ready (Premiere/Final Cut/DaVinci). Map style, terrain, pitch and codec are ignored in this mode.
animator.alpha.preview_hint: Alpha mode active — the map will not be in the final video.
animator.video_load_fail: Preview could not load — use “▶ Play” for the system player.
animator.render_error.title: Render failed
animator.render_error.body: An error occurred during rendering. See traceback and log file below for details.
animator.playwright_missing.title: Render browser missing
animator.playwright_missing.body: The Animator uses a headless Chromium browser to render the 3D Mapbox map for every frame. This browser has not been downloaded yet.
animator.playwright_missing.body2: One-time download (~150 MB). After that rendering works out-of-the-box. Stored in the system cache at:
animator.playwright_missing.installing: Downloading browser (~150 MB, this may take a moment) …
animator.stats.empty_hint: Load a GPX file — track stats will appear here
geotagger.offset.help_tooltip: The camera clock sometimes doesn't match GPS — shift all photo times with the slider, markers move live with it. Tip: filter by a camera above and the slider (and reference photo) apply only to that camera — so you can align two cameras independently.
geotagger.btn.ref_mode_tooltip: 1. Pick a photo, 2. Click on the map where you actually took it, 3. Offset is calculated automatically.
geotagger.toggle.adjust_time: Also adjust photo capture time with offset
geotagger.gpx_nearby.body_single: Load this file as the track?
geotagger.gpx_nearby.body_multi: Which one would you like to load?
geotagger.offset.tz_intro: Only needed if the camera does NOT store the time zone in its metadata (e.g. many Olympus/OM, GoPro). Photos that already store a time zone (phones etc.) stay correct automatically.
geotagger.offset.modal_intro: Negative = camera clock was ahead (subtract). Examples:
geotagger.toast.folder_missing: Last photo folder no longer exists
geotagger.empty.title: Your photos go here
geotagger.tagcheck.title: Will be tagged (uncheck = don't tag)
geotagger.remove.title: Remove from list
geotagger.resync.title: Re-sync to the track by capture time
geotagger.resync.no_track: No track loaded — photo stays without a position for now.
geotagger.badge.oor: Outside the track's time — no track point for this capture time
geotagger.badge.notime: No usable capture time (EXIF) — cannot be matched
geotagger.place.hint: Drag a photo from the list onto the map — its coordinates will be written. Works without a track too.
geotagger.ignore_gps.label: Ignore photo's own GPS
geotagger.ignore_gps.help: Places this camera's photos by capture time on the track — instead of using their embedded GPS. Ideal for phones with imprecise cell-tower location. Applies to the camera filtered above (no filter = default for all).
geotagger.settime.label: Set capture time from track
geotagger.settime.help: Writes the (local) time of the matched track point as the capture time for all matched photos of this camera. This keeps two cameras with mis-set clocks in sync in Lightroom. GPS stays correct. Applies to the camera filtered above (no filter = default for all).
geotagger.place.cmd_hint: Hold ⌘ while dropping to invert this briefly.
geotagger.place.no_track: No track loaded — photos are placed freely.
geotagger.place.hint_snap: Snap to track — release to set
geotagger.place.already_placed: Already on the map — grab the pin directly to move it.
geotagger.track_info.title: Track point
geotagger.empty.text:
Drag photos or a folder from Finder right here,
or use the buttons on the left.
geotagger.banner.ref_mode: 🎯 {name} — click on the map where it actually was
geotagger.banner.ref_mode_generic: 🎯 Click on the map where the photo actually was
geotagger.dnd.photos_hint: Drop photos, folder or GPX here
geotagger.toast.select_photo_first: Pick a photo on the left first
geotagger.toast.all_have_gps: All photos already have GPS. Enable 'Overwrite existing' if needed.
geotagger.summary.title: Summary:
write_modal.title: Start write?
write_modal.backup_path: 📦 Backup ZIP will be created at:
progress_modal.title: Writing GPS …
progress_modal.skipped_info: {n} photos with existing GPS will be skipped.
offset_modal.title: Enter exact offset
offset_modal.help: Value in seconds. Negative = camera clock was ahead (subtract).
signs.hint: Text signs along the route. They appear in the video as soon as the marker reaches the spot.
signs.place_active: Click on the track to place the sign … (Esc cancels)
signs.list_hint: Click a sign (list or map) to open its settings.
signs.move_active: Click on the track to move the sign … (Esc cancels)
photos.hint: Photos with GPS EXIF appear as small thumbnails on the map.
photos.toast_gtg_empty: No Geotagger photos with GPS found.
photos.pick_choice_folder_sub: Scan all photos in a folder
animator.field.spin_tooltip: Constant rotation speed (degrees per second). Works everywhere — intro, animation and hold. Positive = clockwise, negative = counter-clockwise. On globe view: Earth rotates.
animator.kf.spin_tooltip: Rotation speed (degrees per second) from this keyframe. Linearly interpolated to the next KF. 0 = no spin. Positive = clockwise.
animator.field.cinematic_flyto_tooltip: On large zoom jumps (globe→detail), the camera takes a slightly arcing path — like Mapbox' flyTo. Nice cinematic effect, but can feel 'zooms out too far'. Disable for strict linear interpolation.
animator.kf.world_center_tooltip: Center the map on the globe view (pitch=0, zoom=1.5, track midpoint). In KF mode this also creates a world KF at the current scrubber anchor.
animator.kf.world_center_start_tip: Globe view centered on the track START POINT. When zooming in, the start point stays fixed — the earth rotates around it without wandering laterally through the world. In KF mode creates a world KF.
animator.kf.world_center_bbox_tip: Globe view centered on the WORLD CENTER (Greenwich/Equator). Classic front-on Earth view like before — the track sits off-center and the camera flies into it on render. In KF mode creates a world KF.
signs.place_active_free: Click anywhere on the map (e.g. a sight) … (Esc cancels)
signs.move_active_free: Click anywhere on the map to move the sign … (Esc cancels)
signs.bg_none_hint: No background (transparent) — e.g. image without a colored frame
signs.deco_len_hint: Length of the posts for banner / signpost
signs.time_anchor_hint: For out-and-back routes: move the scrubber to the desired moment, then pin it here (resolves the same-place ambiguity).
signs.preview_all_hint: Preview only (for placing) — in the video they still appear per timing.
signs.help: Signs and photos along the route — a photo is a sign with an image. With “📷 Add photos”, photos (with GPS) land automatically at their capture coordinates. Everything appears in the video as the marker reaches its point. In the list: ⠿ drag to reorder, checkbox = on/off, ✎ = edit, ✕ = delete.
signs.apply_all_hint: Apply appearance (shape/colors/font/shadow/size) AND behavior (timing/entry/zoom/show-whole-time) to all others — text/image/position/visibility stay
signs.move_toast_free: 📍 Click the map for the new position — Esc cancels
signs.move_toast_track: 📍 Click the track for the new position — Esc cancels
route.intro: Set a start and destination — a route is computed and animated like a track (camera flight, signs, render included).
route.grob.tooltip: Simplified, coarser line — not as fine-grained as a real hike.
route.no_start: Start missing — enter an address or 📍 click on the map.
route.no_end: Destination missing — enter an address or 📍 click on the map.
route.not_found: %s: address not found — try a different spelling or 📍 click on the map.
modules.reiseroute.name: Travel route
modules.reiseroute.desc: Animate the trip as a route
route.ghost.intro: The loaded GPX (e.g. the hike) as a faint line behind the animated route.
gpxinspect.despike_tip: Scans the whole track for GPS outliers (orange) AND gaps/dropouts (magenta dashed). Shows a preview on the map of what would be healed — then 'Heal all'.
gpxinspect.delete_one_tip: Remove the selected point (anchor A). Also works with Delete/Backspace.
gpxinspect.trim_before_tip: Remove the start of the track up to this point — this point becomes the new start (e.g. cut off the drive in or standing still at the beginning).
gpxinspect.trim_after_tip: Remove everything after this point — this point becomes the new end (e.g. cut off a recording you forgot to stop at the end of the tour).
gpxinspect.sens_tip: How sensitively outliers are detected. Low = only big jumps, high = even small spikes. The marking updates live as you drag.
settings.render.title: Quality & Export
settings.render.crf_hint: Lower = better quality & larger file. 20 is a good default.
settings.render.hint: Applies to the Animator video export. JPEG is ideal for YouTube/web; use PNG only if you truly need lossless frames. Alpha/ProRes renders automatically use lossless frames.
settings.defaults.title: Defaults for new tracks
settings.defaults.help: Each track remembers its own settings. New tracks normally start from the factory defaults. Here you can save your current look (style, colors, overlays, map smoothing, etc.) as your personal default for ALL new tracks. Existing tracks stay unchanged.
settings.defaults.saved: Saved — new tracks now use your look.
settings.defaults.reset_done: Reset — new tracks start from the factory defaults again.
settings.defaults.status_custom: ● Active: new tracks use your own defaults.
settings.defaults.status_factory: New tracks currently use the factory defaults.
settings.force_osm.label: Map engine: force OSM (test)
settings.force_osm.help: Runs the app as if it had no Mapbox token — standard OSM map, no satellite/3D, video render disabled. Your token stays saved, just unused. For testing only.
osm_block.title: Mapbox token required
osm_block.body: This module needs a Mapbox token (for satellite, 3D and rendering). In OSM mode, preview and export are disabled here.
gpxinspect.routing: Finding a route between A and B …
gpxinspect.route_nomatch: No route found — A or B is too far from a road.
geotagger.place.set_time_hint: Only for snapped photos: writes the track point's time as the capture time — ideal for WhatsApp photos with no correct time.
export_gpx.no_track: No track loaded — open a file first.
export_csv.no_track: No track loaded — open a file first.
gpxinspect.profile_help: Straight line = direct line between points. Walking/Cycling/Car = find the real route on the path network (Mapbox, needs internet + token), the track follows the ways. Very large gaps (e.g. flights) always stay straight.
geotagger.wf.title: What gets written into the photo
geotagger.wf.keywords_help: Writes the auto-tag keywords into the photo. Off = the recognition keywords are not written (but stay saved).
geotagger.wf.global_help: Writes the values set in the „Global fields“ modal (author, copyright, credit, website …) onto all visible photos. Off = write none of them without deleting the values.
geotagger.wf.exif_edits_help: Writes the tags you edited by hand or added via „+ field“ in the EXIF tab. Off = your manual EXIF changes are not written (stay saved).
geotagger.addr.hint: This address is written into the photo when tagging (IPTC/XMP). All fields empty = no address.
geotagger.geocode.disabled: Address lookup is turned off in settings.
settings.geocode.title: Address lookup (Geotagger)
settings.geocode.intro: When tagging, the app can determine the address (city, country, street) for each photo location and write it into the photo. This queries an online map service.
settings.geocode.enabled_help: Off = nothing is sent to the internet at all. You can then only type addresses by hand.
settings.geocode.help_auto: uses Mapbox if a token is set above, otherwise Photon. Best choice for most.
settings.geocode.help_mapbox: fastest (~10 queries/sec, 100k/month free). Uses your Mapbox token from above.
settings.geocode.help_photon: OpenStreetMap data via Komoot, no token needed, fairly quick.
settings.geocode.help_nominatim: straight from OpenStreetMap, no token, but only ~1 query/second — slower with many photos.
geotagger.mode.label: When a photo already has data
geotagger.mode.fill: Keep it, only add what's missing
geotagger.mode.skip: Skip photos that already have GPS
geotagger.mode.help: “Add missing” leaves existing GPS untouched and only adds what's missing (e.g. address, facing direction). A location already stored in the photo then takes priority over the time match.
geotagger.mode.row_fill: Keep GPS, get missing data added
geotagger.exif.add_hint: Collected as a pending change and written into the photo on “Write tags” (with backup).
geotagger.exif.f.title: Title
geotagger.exif.f.headline: Headline
geotagger.exif.pending_note: {n} change(s) — saved when you click „Write tags“
geotagger.exif.unsaved_banner: {n} unsaved EXIF change(s) in {p} photo(s) — save them with „Write tags“
geotagger.gf.title: Global fields (author, copyright …)
geotagger.gf.hint: These fields are written to all visible/checked photos when you click „Write tags“ (EXIF + IPTC + XMP). Set once, they stay saved. Empty = don't write.
geotagger.gf.status_set: ✓ {n} field(s) set — written to all visible photos
geotagger.autotag.title: Image recognition running …
